growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.strain: B13 (wild-type P. knackmussii; strain code inferred)

up.method: HPLC; only 60% of 2-FBz processed, conversion stalled after 20 h

sec.method: HPLC/19F-NMR; Y_P/S=0.14 mol/mol; 2-FMA to ~1 mM alongside ccMA

growth_records.cite: Wirth, Nicolas T. et al. Combinatorial pathway balancing provides biosynthetic access to 2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate in engineered Pseudomonas putida. Chem Catalysis (2021). DOI:10.1016/j.checat.2021.09.002. [PMC8711041]
growth_records.snippet: P. knackmussii only processed 60% of the haloaromatic, and the conversion stalled after 20 h. 2-FMA was produced to just 1 mM together with ccMA... For both species, 2-FMA amounted to ca. 14% of the 2-FBz consumed (i.e., molar product yield
growth_records.notes: P. knackmussii on 10 mM 2-FBz + 30 mM glucose: 60% 2-FBz processed, Y_P/S(2-FMA)=0.14 mol/mol. Strain code B13 inferred (P. knackmussii reference/type strain); not stated explicitly in body text. Does not grow on benzoate as sole carbon source; grows on glucose ~2.5x slower than P. putida. Shaken-flask, 30C, pH 7.0.

growth_records.strain: B13 (wild-type P. knackmussii; strain code inferred)

up.method: HPLC; partially consumed, initial 3-FC increase reconsumed by 20 h

growth_records.cite: Wirth, Nicolas T. et al. Combinatorial pathway balancing provides biosynthetic access to 2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate in engineered Pseudomonas putida. Chem Catalysis (2021). DOI:10.1016/j.checat.2021.09.002. [PMC8711041]
growth_records.snippet: P. knackmussii partially converted 3-FBz (without prior incubation with chlorobenzoate) concomitant with an initial increase of 3-FC, which was reconsumed until 20 h and transformed into 2-FMA, ultimately leading to Y P/S = 0.55 mol mol-1.
growth_records.notes: P. knackmussii on 10 mM 3-FBz + 30 mM glucose: Y_P/S(2-FMA)=0.55 mol/mol (higher than wild-type P. putida 0.41 because P. knackmussii detoxifies 3-FC to 2-FMA better). Strain code B13 inferred. Shaken-flask, 30C, pH 7.0, 250 rpm.
